mcqs.question: Which keyword is used to define a structure in C?
mcqs.explanation: The 'struct' keyword is used to define a structure in C. It creates a composite data type that groups variables of different types.

mcqs.question: What will be the output of the following code? struct Book { char title[20]; int pages; float price; }; printf("%d", sizeof(struct Book)); Assume char=1, int=4, float=4 bytes with no padding.
mcqs.explanation: title[20] = 20 bytes, pages (int) = 4 bytes, price (float) = 4 bytes. Total = 20 + 4 + 4 = 28 bytes (without padding).

mcqs.question: Which operator is used to access members of a structure variable?
mcqs.explanation: The dot operator (.) is used to access structure members when working with structure variables directly. The arrow operator (->) is used when working with pointers to structures.

mcqs.question: What is the correct way to declare a variable of a structure defined as: struct Employee { char name[30]; int id; };
mcqs.explanation: When using struct, you must use the 'struct' keyword along with the tag name to declare variables: struct Employee emp;

mcqs.question: What is a nested structure?

options.text: A structure containing another structure as a member

mcqs.explanation: A nested structure is a structure that has another structure as one of its members. This is useful for representing complex data like dates within employee records.

mcqs.question: What is the advantage of using typedef with structures?

options.text: It allows omitting the 'struct' keyword when declaring variables

mcqs.explanation: typedef creates an alias for the structure type, allowing cleaner syntax. Instead of 'struct Point p1;', you can write 'Point p1;' after using typedef.

mcqs.question: What will be the value of sizeof for a structure with members: char a; int b; char c; on a 32-bit system with 4-byte int alignment?
mcqs.explanation: Due to structure padding: char a (1 byte) + 3 bytes padding, int b (4 bytes), char c (1 byte) + 3 bytes padding = 12 bytes total.

mcqs.question: Which of the following is NOT a valid way to access structure members?
mcqs.explanation: Structure members are accessed using dot (.) or arrow (->) operators, not array subscript notation. structure[member] is invalid syntax.

mcqs.question: What is an anonymous structure?

options.text: A structure without a tag name, directly declaring variables
A structure with all members as private
A structure that cannot be modified

mcqs.explanation: An anonymous structure is declared without a tag name: struct { int x; } a, b; It cannot be reused to declare other variables later.

mcqs.question: Can structure members be of different data types?

options.text: No, all members must be of the same type
Yes, structures can have members of different data types

mcqs.explanation: The primary advantage of structures is that they can group variables of different data types (int, float, char, arrays, pointers, other structures, etc.)

mcqs.question: What is the correct order for accessing a nested structure member? Given: struct Date { int day; }; struct Person { struct Date birthDate; } p;
mcqs.explanation: For nested structures, use chained dot operators: p.birthDate.day accesses the day member of the birthDate structure within person p.

mcqs.question: What is structure padding?

options.text: Extra bytes added for array allocation
Extra bytes added between members for memory alignment
A technique to reduce structure size
A method to initialize structure members

mcqs.explanation: Structure padding is the addition of extra bytes between members to align data on memory boundaries for faster access. This makes sizeof(struct) potentially larger than the sum of member sizes.

mcqs.question: How do you initialize a structure at the time of declaration?
mcqs.explanation: Structures can be initialized using curly braces: struct Student s = { "John", 20, 3.5 }; Values are assigned in the order members were declared.

mcqs.question: Which of the following is true about structure members?

options.text: They must be initialized in order
They can be of any data type including other structures

mcqs.explanation: Structure members can be of any data type including primitive types, arrays, pointers, other structures, and even functions (through function pointers).

mcqs.question: What happens if you declare a structure variable without initialization?

options.text: It is automatically set to zero (if global/static)
It cannot be used in calculations

mcqs.explanation: Local structure variables (like local variables) contain garbage values if not initialized. Global/static structures are automatically initialized to zero.
